title: Diagnosis of small pulmonary lesions by transbronchial lung biopsy with radial endobronchial ultrasound and virtual bronchoscopic navigation versus CT-guided transthoracic needle biopsy: a systematic review and meta-analysis
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"k37l","text":"Background","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cd8ae","text":"Advances in bronchoscopy and CT-guided lung biopsy have improved the evaluation of small pulmonary lesions (PLs), leading to an increase in preoperative histological diagnosis.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ddp2l","text":"We aimed to evaluate the efficacy and safety of transbronchial lung biopsy using radial endobronchial ultrasound and virtual bronchoscopic navigation (TBLB-rEBUS\u0026VBN) with CT-guided transthoracic needle biopsy (CT-TNB).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f6b3l","text":"Method","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":6}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6b92d","text":"A systematic search was performed in five electronic databases, including MEDLINE, EMBASE, Cochrane Library Central Register of Controlled Trials, Web of Science, and Scopus, for relevant studies in May 2016; the selected articles were assessed using meta-analysis. The articles were limited to those published after 2000 that studied small PLs ≤ 3 cm in diameter.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8qhmm","text":"Result","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":6}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ccsk8","text":"From 7345 records, 9 articles on the bronchoscopic (BR) approach and 15 articles on the percutaneous (PC) approach were selected. The pooled diagnostic yield was 75% (95% confidence interval [CI], 69-80) using the BR approach and 93% (95% CI, 90-96) using the PC approach. For PLs ≤ 2 cm, the PC approach was superior to the BR approach. However, for PLs \u003e 2 cm but ≤ 3 cm, the diagnostic yield using the BR approach was increased to 81% (95% CI, 75-85). Complications of pneumothorax and hemorrhage were rare with the BR approach but common with the PC approach.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7d9u8","text":"Conclusion","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fjiql","text":"CT-TNB was superior to TBLB-rEBUS\u0026VBN for the evaluation of small PLs. However, for lesions greater than 2 cm, the BR approach may be considered considering its diagnostic yield of over 80% and the low risk of procedure-related complications.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines:
{"blocks":[{"key":"e0jqe","text":"Inclusion and exclusion criteria","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":32}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7fs2i","text":"The following inclusion criteria were used to select studies on TBLB-rEBUS&VBN: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a9ko2","text":"1. rEBUS with a GS was used for the diagnosis of peripheral pulmonary lesions (PPLs).
 2. Virtual bronchoscopy was used as a navigational method.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c598c","text":"3. A PPL was defined as an endobronchial lesion not detected by bronchoscopy, and the size of these lesions was limited to ≤ 3 cm in diameter.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3e83d","text":"4. The final diagnosis was confirmed using the biopsy specimen or surgical specimen; in cases of diagnostic ambiguity, the final diagnosis was established at the clinical follow-up.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"934a2","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8v5i9","text":"The following inclusion criteria were used to select studies on CT-TNB:
 1. CT-guided transthoracic needle aspiration or biopsy, including conventional CT-guided transthoracic needle biopsy, CT fluoroscopy-guided transthoracic biopsy, and C-arm cone-beam CT-guided transthoracic biopsy, was used for the diagnosis of PL.
 2. The biopsy target was a small PL ≤ 3 cm in diameter for which bronchoscopic biopsy was considered unfeasible based on the imaging information. The target lesion was described as a PPL, solitary pulmonary nodule (SPN), pulmonary nodule, ground glass opacity, or PL.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a10l4","text":"3. The final diagnosis was confirmed using the biopsy specimen or surgical specimen; in cases of diagnostic ambiguity, the final diagnosis was established at the clinical follow-up.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c61ln","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6chu4","text":"The following exclusion criteria were used when selecting studies on TBLB-rEBUS&VBN or CT-TNB:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4mbgb","text":"1. Studies using non-human subjects, studies analyzing other methods of biopsy, studies written in a language other than English, or studies of an inappropriate type (case report, case series, letter, and review);","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"aigmf","text":"2. Central bronchial lesions evaluated by routine bronchoscopy;","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3p5bp","text":"3. Electromagnetic navigational bronchoscopy (ENB) procedure in TBLB;","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9kjod","text":"4. Studies devoted to topics other than diagnostic outcomes with or without adverse events.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"7o2rk","text":"When comparing the diagnostic yields of different methods, the lesions should be limited to those ≤ 3 cm in diameter, as established by previous publications. In this analysis, PLs up to 3 cm in diameter were considered.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dek3c","text":"The purpose of this meta-analysis was to compare the efficacy and safety of TBLB with rEBUS and VBN (TBLB-rEBUS\u0026VBN), a BR approach, with CT-TNB, a PC approach, for tissue diagnosis of small PLs.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
creator: Jung Chang
